subject: Biology
title: Mitochondria: The Powerhouse
description: A fast breakdown of Mitochondria: The Powerhouse for builders.

hook.text: Mitochondria burn glucose with oxygen and produce ATP, the energy currency of life.

buildup.text: Each human cell holds hundreds to thousands of mitochondria, based on energy demand.

discovery.text: Mitochondria have their own DNA—a circular genome inherited only from your mother.

twist.text: Billions of years ago, a larger cell swallowed a bacterium. They partnered instead of fighting.

climax.text: That ancient partnership still runs. Every breath you take fuels your mitochondria.

punchline.text: A swallowed bacterium still powers your body.

quiz.question: Where does mitochondrial DNA come from?
